161	// BuildReviewPrompt builds the complete review prompt for a bead implementation
162	// using default caps. Callers needing overflow detection use
163	// BuildReviewPromptBounded.
164	func BuildReviewPrompt(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef) string {
165		return BuildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, rev, diff, projectRoot, refs, BuildReviewPromptOptions{Caps: evidence.DefaultCaps()}).Prompt
166	}

168	// BuildReviewPromptBounded assembles the review prompt under byte caps using
169	// the cli/internal/evidence primitives. Governing documents are read via
170	// evidence.ReadFileClamped, the diff is decomposed and clamped via
171	// evidence.ClampDiff, and per-file inlining is bounded by
172	// Caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes. The minimum evidence floor (bead title,
173	// description, acceptance, notes, and the full changed-file inventory) is
174	// preserved verbatim regardless of cap pressure (FEAT-022 §5).
175	func BuildReviewPromptBounded(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef, opts BuildReviewPromptOptions) BuildReviewPromptResult {
176		caps := opts.Caps
177		if caps.MaxPromptBytes == 0 {
178			caps = evidence.DefaultCaps()
179		}
180	
181		var sb strings.Builder
182		sections := []evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}
183	
184		sb.WriteString("<bead-review>\n")
185	
186		// ── Bead section (floor) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
187		beadStart := sb.Len()
188		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <bead id=%q iter=%d>\n", b.ID, iter)
189		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <title>%s</title>\n", reviewXMLEscape(strings.TrimSpace(b.Title)))
190	
191		if desc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Description); desc != "" {
192			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <description>\n%s\n    </description>\n", reviewXMLEscape(desc))
193		} else {
194			sb.WriteString("    <description/>\n")
195		}
196	
197		if acc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Acceptance); acc != "" {
198			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <acceptance>\n%s\n    </acceptance>\n", reviewXMLEscape(acc))
199		} else {
200			sb.WriteString("    <acceptance/>\n")
201		}
202	
203		if notes := strings.TrimSpace(b.Notes); notes != "" {
204			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <notes>\n%s\n    </notes>\n", reviewXMLEscape(notes))
205		}
206	
207		if len(b.Labels) > 0 {
208			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <labels>%s</labels>\n", reviewXMLEscape(strings.Join(b.Labels, ", ")))
209		}
210	
211		sb.WriteString("  </bead>\n\n")
212		sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
213			Name:          "bead",
214			BytesIncluded: sb.Len() - beadStart,
215			SelectedItems: []string{"bead"},
216		})
217	
218		// ── Changed-file inventory (floor) ──────────────────────────────────────
219		allFiles := evidence.DecomposeDiff(diff)
220		if len(allFiles) > 0 {
221			invStart := sb.Len()
222			sb.WriteString("  <changed-files>\n")
223			paths := make([]string, 0, len(allFiles))
224			for _, f := range allFiles {
225				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <file>%s</file>\n", reviewXMLEscape(f.Path))
226				paths = append(paths, f.Path)
227			}
228			sb.WriteString("  </changed-files>\n\n")
229			sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
230				Name:          "changed-files",
231				BytesIncluded: sb.Len() - invStart,
232				SelectedItems: paths,
233			})
234		}
235	
236		// ── Governing docs section (clamped per-doc) ────────────────────────────
237		sb.WriteString("  <governing>\n")
238		if len(refs) == 0 {
239			sb.WriteString("    <note>No governing documents found. Evaluate the diff against the acceptance criteria alone.</note>\n")
240		} else {
241			for _, ref := range refs {
242				if ref.Title != "" {
243					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref id=%q path=%q title=%q>\n", ref.ID, ref.Path, ref.Title)
244				} else {
245					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref id=%q path=%q>\n", ref.ID, ref.Path)
246				}
247				docPath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, filepath.FromSlash(ref.Path))
248				content, truncated, originalBytes, readErr := evidence.ReadFileClamped(docPath, caps.MaxGoverningDocBytes)
249				if readErr != nil {
250					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <note>Could not read %s: %s</note>\n", ref.Path, readErr)
251					sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
252						Name:             "governing:" + ref.ID,
253						TruncationReason: "read_error",
254						OmittedItems:     []string{ref.Path},
255					})
256				} else {
257					txt := strings.TrimSpace(string(content))
258					if truncated {
259						txt += evidence.TruncationMarker
260					}
261					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <content>\n%s\n      </content>\n", txt)
262					sec := evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
263						Name:          "governing:" + ref.ID,
264						BytesIncluded: len(content),
265						SelectedItems: []string{ref.Path},
266					}
267					if truncated {
268						sec.TruncationReason = "governing_doc_cap"
269						sec.BytesOmitted = int(originalBytes) - len(content)
270					}
271					sections = append(sections, sec)
272				}
273				sb.WriteString("    </ref>\n")
274			}
275		}
276		sb.WriteString("  </governing>\n\n")
277	
278		// ── Diff section (ranked, per-file inlined cap, total clamp) ────────────
279		rankedDiff := rankAndDegradeDiffForReview(allFiles, diff, b, refs, caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes)
280		clampedDiff, diffSection := evidence.ClampDiff(rankedDiff, caps.MaxDiffBytes)
281		diffSection.Name = "diff"
282		sections = append(sections, diffSection)
283		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <diff rev=%q>\n%s\n  </diff>\n\n", rev, strings.TrimRight(clampedDiff, "\n"))
284	
285		// ── Instructions section ────────────────────────────────────────────────
286		instructions := strings.ReplaceAll(beadReviewInstructions, "<bead-id>", b.ID)
287		instructions = strings.ReplaceAll(instructions, "<N>", fmt.Sprintf("%d", iter))
288		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <instructions>\n%s\n  </instructions>\n", reviewXMLEscape(instructions))
289	
290		sb.WriteString("</bead-review>\n")
291	
292		out := sb.String()
293		return BuildReviewPromptResult{
294			Prompt:   out,
295			Overflow: len(out) > caps.MaxPromptBytes,
296			Sections: sections,
297		}
298	}

300	// rankAndDegradeDiffForReview reorders diff files so that AC-referenced files
301	// appear first, then files matching governing-ref paths, then others; and
302	// degrades any individual file body that exceeds maxInlinedFileBytes to
303	// "stat + hunk headers only" so a single oversize file cannot dominate the
304	// diff section. The returned string is a re-serialized unified diff suitable
305	// for evidence.ClampDiff. FEAT-022 §1.
306	func rankAndDegradeDiffForReview(files []evidence.DiffFile, originalDiff string, b *bead.Bead, refs []GoverningRef, maxInlinedFileBytes int) string {
307		if len(files) == 0 {
308			return originalDiff
309		}
310		rank := func(f evidence.DiffFile) int {
311			if f.Path == "" {
312				return 3
313			}
314			if mentionsPathLike(b.Acceptance, f.Path) {
315				return 0
316			}
317			for _, r := range refs {
318				if pathsRelated(r.Path, f.Path) {
319					return 1
320				}
321			}
322			return 2
323		}
324		ordered := make([]evidence.DiffFile, len(files))
325		copy(ordered, files)
326		sort.SliceStable(ordered, func(i, j int) bool { return rank(ordered[i]) < rank(ordered[j]) })
327	
328		var b2 strings.Builder
329		for _, f := range ordered {
330			body := f.Body
331			if maxInlinedFileBytes > 0 && len(body) > maxInlinedFileBytes {
332				body = degradeDiffFileBody(f)
333			}
334			b2.WriteString(body)
335		}
336		return b2.String()
337	}
338	
339	// degradeDiffFileBody returns a stat + hunk-headers only rendering of a diff
340	// file (FEAT-022 §1: large-file degradation). Used when a single file's body
341	// exceeds Caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes — preserves enough structure for the
342	// reviewer to know what changed without inlining the full content.
343	func degradeDiffFileBody(f evidence.DiffFile) string {
344		var b strings.Builder
345		b.WriteString("diff --git a/")
346		b.WriteString(f.Path)
347		b.WriteString(" b/")
348		b.WriteString(f.Path)
349		b.WriteString("\n")
350		if f.Stat != "" {
351			b.WriteString(f.Stat)
352			b.WriteString("\n")
353		}
354		for _, h := range f.HunkHeaders {
355			b.WriteString(h)
356			b.WriteString("\n")
357		}
358		b.WriteString("[…file body omitted by ddx evidence cap…]\n")
359		return b.String()
360	}

362	// mentionsPathLike returns true when text mentions the file path or its
363	// basename (a coarse heuristic — the reviewer only needs ranking, not
364	// exact symbolic resolution).
365	func mentionsPathLike(text, p string) bool {
366		if text == "" || p == "" {
367			return false
368		}
369		if strings.Contains(text, p) {
370			return true
371		}
372		base := path.Base(p)
373		return base != "" && base != "." && strings.Contains(text, base)
374	}
375	
376	// pathsRelated returns true when refPath and filePath share a directory
377	// prefix or basename, used to associate diff files with governing
378	// documents.
379	func pathsRelated(refPath, filePath string) bool {
380		if refPath == "" || filePath == "" {
381			return false
382		}
383		refDir := path.Dir(refPath)
384		if refDir != "" && refDir != "." && strings.HasPrefix(filePath, refDir+"/") {
385			return true
386		}
387		if path.Base(refPath) == path.Base(filePath) {
388			return true
389		}
390		return false
391	}

393	// reviewXMLEscape escapes &, <, and > for inclusion in XML text content.
394	func reviewXMLEscape(s string) string {
395		var buf bytes.Buffer
396		buf.WriteString(strings.NewReplacer(
397			"&", "&",
398			"<", "<",
399			">", ">",
400		).Replace(s))
401		return buf.String()
402	}

429	// ReviewBead implements BeadReviewer.
430	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) ReviewBead(ctx context.Context, beadID, resultRev, implHarness, _ string) (*ReviewResult, error) {
431		b, err := r.BeadStore.Get(beadID)
432		if err != nil {
433			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: get bead %s: %w", beadID, err)
434		}
435	
436		// Fetch the git diff for the commit being reviewed.
437		diff, err := r.gitShow(resultRev)
438		if err != nil {
439			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: git show %s: %w", resultRev, err)
440		}
441	
442		// Resolve governing document references.
443		refs := ResolveGoverningRefs(r.ProjectRoot, b)
444	
445		// Determine iteration number from bead events.
446		iter := 1
447	
448		// Build the review prompt under evidence caps.
449		caps := r.Caps
450		if caps.MaxPromptBytes == 0 {
451			caps = evidence.DefaultCaps()
452		}
453		built := BuildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, resultRev, diff, r.ProjectRoot, refs, BuildReviewPromptOptions{Caps: caps})
454		prompt := built.Prompt
455		attemptID := GenerateAttemptID()
456		artifacts, err := createArtifactBundle(r.ProjectRoot, r.ProjectRoot, attemptID)
457		if err != nil {
458			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: create artifact bundle: %w", err)
459		}
460		if err := os.WriteFile(artifacts.PromptAbs, []byte(prompt), 0o644); err != nil {
461			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: write prompt artifact: %w", err)
462		}
463	
464		// Pre-dispatch short-circuit (FEAT-022 §7): if the assembled prompt
465		// exceeds MaxPromptBytes after all per-section trimming, skip the
466		// provider dispatch and return a typed review-error: context_overflow.
467		// The bead is NOT closed — the loop's review-error event-append path
468		// records the failure and leaves the bead open for retry.
469		if built.Overflow {
470			baseRev := resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev)
471			reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
472				Verdict:         VerdictBlock,
473				Error:           evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
474				Rationale:       evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
475				ReviewerHarness: r.Harness,
476				ReviewerModel:   r.Model,
477				BaseRev:         baseRev,
478				ResultRev:       resultRev,
479				ExecutionDir:    artifacts.DirRel,
480			}
481			_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
482				Harness:      r.Harness,
483				Model:        r.Model,
484				BaseRev:      baseRev,
485				ResultRev:    resultRev,
486				Verdict:      string(VerdictBlock),
487				BeadID:       beadID,
488				ExecutionDir: artifacts.DirRel,
489			}, reviewArtifactResult{
490				Verdict: string(VerdictBlock),
491				Error:   evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
492			})
493			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %s (assembled prompt %d bytes exceeds cap %d; see %s)",
494				evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow, len(prompt), caps.MaxPromptBytes, artifacts.DirRel)
495		}
496	
497		// Resolve reviewer harness and model.
498		reviewHarness := r.Harness
499		if reviewHarness == "" {
500			if implHarness != "" {
501				reviewHarness = implHarness
502			} else {
503				reviewHarness = "claude" // default reviewer harness
504			}
505		}
506		reviewModel := r.Model
507		if reviewModel == "" {
508			reviewModel = ResolveModelTier(reviewHarness, SelectReviewerTier(escalation.TierSmart))
509		}
510	
511		start := time.Now()
512		runOpts := RunOptions{
513			Context: ctx,
514			Harness: reviewHarness,
515			Model:   reviewModel,
516			Prompt:  prompt,
517			WorkDir: r.ProjectRoot,
518		}
519		result, runErr := r.dispatchReviewRun(ctx, runOpts)
520	
521		durationMS := int(time.Since(start).Milliseconds())
522		if runErr != nil {
523			// Transport-class failure (FEAT-022 §12): network or provider-side
524			// error. Surface as a typed review-error so the loop classifies and
525			// counts it correctly, rather than masquerading as a BLOCK verdict.
526			reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
527				Verdict:         VerdictBlock,
528				Rationale:       runErr.Error(),
529				Error:           evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport,
530				ReviewerHarness: reviewHarness,
531				ReviewerModel:   reviewModel,
532				BaseRev:         resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev),
533				ResultRev:       resultRev,
534				ExecutionDir:    artifacts.DirRel,
535				DurationMS:      durationMS,
536			}
537			_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
538				Harness:      reviewHarness,
539				Model:        reviewModel,
540				BaseRev:      reviewRes.BaseRev,
541				ResultRev:    resultRev,
542				Verdict:      string(reviewRes.Verdict),
543				BeadID:       beadID,
544				ExecutionDir: artifacts.DirRel,
545			}, reviewArtifactResult{
546				Verdict:   string(reviewRes.Verdict),
547				Rationale: reviewRes.Rationale,
548				Error:     reviewRes.Error,
549			})
550			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %s: %w", evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport, runErr)
551		}
552	
553		actualHarness := reviewHarness
554		actualModel := reviewModel
555		if result != nil {
556			if result.Harness != "" {
557				actualHarness = result.Harness
558			}
559			if result.Model != "" {
560				actualModel = result.Model
561			}
562			durationMS = result.DurationMS
563		}
564	
565		output := ""
566		sessionID := ""
567		if result != nil {
568			output = result.Output
569			sessionID = result.AgentSessionID
570		}
571		// Strict JSON parse: replaces the legacy markdown extractor that silently
572		// pulled "BLOCK" from the prompt's options-header line whenever the model
573		// echoed it back (the upstream-report regression). On parse error we emit
574		// a typed review-error class — the execute-loop reopens the bead for
575		// retry rather than mis-recording a BLOCK verdict.
576		parsed, parseErr := ParseReviewVerdict([]byte(output))
577		var strictVerdict Verdict
578		var rationale string
579		var findings []Finding
580		if parseErr == nil {
581			strictVerdict = parsed.Verdict
582			rationale = strings.TrimSpace(parsed.Summary)
583			findings = parsed.Findings
584			if rationale == "" && len(findings) > 0 {
585				parts := make([]string, 0, len(findings))
586				for _, f := range findings {
587					line := strings.TrimSpace(f.Summary)
588					if line == "" {
589						continue
590					}
591					if f.Location != "" {
592						line = f.Location + ": " + line
593					}
594					parts = append(parts, line)
595				}
596				rationale = strings.Join(parts, "\n")
597			}
598		}
599		baseRev := resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev)
600		reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
601			Verdict:         strictVerdict,
602			Rationale:       rationale,
603			RawOutput:       output,
604			ReviewerHarness: actualHarness,
605			ReviewerModel:   actualModel,
606			SessionID:       sessionID,
607			BaseRev:         baseRev,
608			ResultRev:       resultRev,
609			ExecutionDir:    artifacts.DirRel,
610			DurationMS:      durationMS,
611		}
612		_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
613			Harness:      actualHarness,
614			Model:        actualModel,
615			SessionID:    sessionID,
616			BaseRev:      baseRev,
617			ResultRev:    resultRev,
618			Verdict:      string(strictVerdict),
619			BeadID:       beadID,
620			ExecutionDir: artifacts.DirRel,
621		}, reviewArtifactResult{
622			Verdict:   string(strictVerdict),
623			Rationale: rationale,
624			Findings:  findings,
625			Error:     reviewRes.Error,
626		})
627		if parseErr != nil {
628			// FEAT-022 §12: distinguish provider_empty (zero bytes) from
629			// unparseable (text without a recognizable JSON contract). Operators
630			// triage these differently — provider_empty often signals a context-
631			// window or backend availability issue, while unparseable signals a
632			// reviewer-prompt or output-format drift.
633			class := evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
634			if strings.TrimSpace(output) == "" {
635				class = evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty
636			}
637			reviewRes.Error = class
638			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %s: %w (raw output %d bytes; see %s)", class, parseErr, len(output), artifacts.DirRel)
639		}
640		return reviewRes, nil
641	}
642	
643	// dispatchReviewRun resolves how the review invocation should be executed.
644	// Resolution order matches dispatchAgentRun:
645	//  1. r.Runner (test injection seam) — used directly via Runner.Run.
646	//  2. r.Service (pre-built service) — used via RunViaServiceWith.
647	//  3. Fallback: construct a fresh service via NewServiceFromWorkDir(ProjectRoot)
648	//     and dispatch via RunViaServiceWith.
649	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) dispatchReviewRun(ctx context.Context, runOpts RunOptions) (*Result, error) {
650		if r.Runner != nil {
651			return r.Runner.Run(runOpts)
652		}
653		svc := r.Service
654		if svc == nil {
655			built, err := NewServiceFromWorkDir(r.ProjectRoot)
656			if err != nil {
657				return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: build agent service: %w", err)
658			}
659			svc = built
660		}
661		return RunViaServiceWith(ctx, svc, r.ProjectRoot, runOpts)
662	}
663	
664	// gitShow runs `git show <rev>` with pathspec exclusions for execution-
665	// evidence noise so the review prompt's <diff> section stays bounded even
666	// when an old commit tracked a multi-thousand-line session log. See
667	// EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs and ddx-39e27896 for the regression.
668	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) gitShow(rev string) (string, error) {
669		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 60*time.Second)
670		defer cancel()
671		args := append([]string{"show", rev, "--", "."}, EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs()...)
672		out, err := internalgit.Command(ctx, r.ProjectRoot, args...).Output()
673		if err != nil {
674			return "", err
675		}
676		return string(out), nil
677	}
678	
679	func resolveReviewBaseRev(projectRoot, resultRev string) string {
680		if resultRev == "" {
681			return ""
682		}
683		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "rev-parse", resultRev+"^").Output()
684		if err != nil {
685			return ""
686		}
687		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
688	}
689	
690	func writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts *executeBeadArtifacts, manifest reviewArtifactManifest, result reviewArtifactResult) error {
691		if artifacts == nil {
692			return nil
693		}
694		if err := writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.ManifestAbs, manifest); err != nil {
695			return err
696		}
697		return writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.ResultAbs, result)
698	}
699	
700	func ReadReviewArtifactResult(path string) (*ReviewResult, error) {
701		//evidence:allow-unbounded — review result artifacts are small JSON
702		// documents written by writeReviewArtifacts; bounded by construction.
703		raw, err := os.ReadFile(path)
704		if err != nil {
705			return nil, err
706		}
707		var artifact reviewArtifactResult
708		if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &artifact); err != nil {
709			return nil, err
710		}
711		return &ReviewResult{
712			Verdict:   Verdict(artifact.Verdict),
713			Rationale: artifact.Rationale,
714			PerAC:     artifact.PerAC,
715			Error:     artifact.Error,
716		}, nil
717	}

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"strings"
7	
8		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/evidence"
9	)
10	
11	// GradeOptions configures a grading invocation.
12	type GradeOptions struct {
13		Grader string // harness to use for grading
14		Rubric string // custom rubric text (replaces default)
15		// Caps configures per-section evidence caps used when assembling the
16		// grading prompt (FEAT-022 §5/§7). When zero-valued, evidence.DefaultCaps
17		// applies.
18		Caps evidence.Caps
19		// OnEvent, when non-nil, receives a grading event for the pre-dispatch
20		// short-circuit (FEAT-022 §7) and any post-dispatch outcome the caller
21		// wires up. The pre-dispatch overflow event has Kind set to
22		// evidence.OutcomeCompareContextOverflow ("compare-error: context_overflow")
23		// and ProviderDispatchCount of zero — the provider was not invoked.
24		OnEvent func(GradingEvent)
25	}
26	
27	// GradingEvent is the structured outcome record emitted by GradeFn for the
28	// grading-side telemetry channel. Kind carries the canonical outcome class
29	// (FEAT-022 §12 mirror for the grading path) and ProviderDispatchCount tracks
30	// whether the grader actually invoked the provider — Stage F aggregates on
31	// these fields.
32	type GradingEvent struct {
33		Kind                  string                             `json:"kind"`
34		Outcome               string                             `json:"outcome,omitempty"`
35		PromptBytes           int                                `json:"prompt_bytes"`
36		BudgetBytes           int                                `json:"budget_bytes"`
37		ProviderDispatchCount int                                `json:"provider_dispatch_count"`
38		Sections              []evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection `json:"sections,omitempty"`
39	}
40	
41	const defaultGradingRubric = `You are evaluating agent outputs for correctness, quality, and completeness.
42	
43	For each arm, provide a JSON grade with:
44	- "arm": the harness name
45	- "score": integer 0-10
46	- "max_score": 10
47	- "pass": true if score >= 7
48	- "rationale": brief explanation
49	
50	Respond with ONLY a JSON object: {"arms": [<grade>, ...]}`
51	
52	const gradingClosingInstruction = "Grade the following comparison arms. Respond with JSON only."
53	
54	// buildGradingPromptResult is the structured outcome of buildGradingPromptBounded.
55	// Overflow is true when, after all per-section trimming, the assembled prompt
56	// still exceeds Caps.MaxPromptBytes; callers MUST NOT dispatch in that case
57	// (FEAT-022 §7).
58	type buildGradingPromptResult struct {
59		Prompt   string
60		Overflow bool
61		Sections []evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection
62	}
63	
64	// GradeFn sends a comparison record to a grading harness and returns
65	// structured grades per arm.
66	//
67	// Pre-dispatch short-circuit (FEAT-022 §7): when the assembled prompt exceeds
68	// Caps.MaxPromptBytes after all bounded-evidence trimming, GradeFn does NOT
69	// dispatch to the provider. It emits a GradingEvent whose Kind is
70	// evidence.OutcomeCompareContextOverflow and returns a typed error. The same
71	// outcome class is mirrored from the reviewer path so Stage F can aggregate
72	// across both surfaces.
73	func GradeFn(r *Runner, record *ComparisonRecord, opts GradeOptions) ([]ComparisonGrade, error) {
74		if opts.Grader == "" {
75			return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent: grader harness is required")
76		}
77	
78		caps := opts.Caps
79		if caps.MaxPromptBytes == 0 {
80			caps = evidence.DefaultCaps()
81		}
82	
83		built := buildGradingPromptBounded(record, opts.Rubric, caps)
84	
85		if built.Overflow {
86			if opts.OnEvent != nil {
87				opts.OnEvent(GradingEvent{
88					Kind:                  evidence.OutcomeCompareContextOverflow,
89					Outcome:               evidence.OutcomeCompareContextOverflow,
90					PromptBytes:           len(built.Prompt),
91					BudgetBytes:           caps.MaxPromptBytes,
92					ProviderDispatchCount: 0,
93					Sections:              built.Sections,
94				})
95			}
96			return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent: %s (assembled prompt %d bytes exceeds cap %d)",
97				evidence.OutcomeCompareContextOverflow, len(built.Prompt), caps.MaxPromptBytes)
98		}
99	
100		// Run the grading harness
101		result, err := r.Run(RunOptions{
102			Harness: opts.Grader,
103			Prompt:  built.Prompt,
104		})
105		if err != nil {
106			return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent: grading failed: %w", err)
107		}
108		if result.ExitCode != 0 {
109			return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent: grader returned exit code %d: %s", result.ExitCode, result.Error)
110		}
111	
112		// Parse the grading response
113		grades, err := parseGrades(result.Output)
114		if err != nil {
115			return nil, err
116		}
117	
118		return grades, nil
119	}
120	
121	// buildGradingPrompt is the legacy entry point preserved for callers that do
122	// not need overflow detection. It assembles the grading prompt under
123	// evidence.DefaultCaps and returns only the prompt text.
124	func buildGradingPrompt(record *ComparisonRecord, customRubric string) string {
125		return buildGradingPromptBounded(record, customRubric, evidence.DefaultCaps()).Prompt
126	}
127	
128	// buildGradingPromptBounded constructs the grading prompt under byte caps using
129	// the cli/internal/evidence primitives. Per-arm Output, PostRunOut, and
130	// ToolCalls are clamped via evidence.ClampOutput; per-arm Diff is decomposed
131	// (evidence.DecomposeDiff) and bounded via evidence.ClampDiff.
132	//
133	// Minimum evidence floor (FEAT-022 §5): the rubric, the task prompt, and each
134	// arm's identity (harness, model) are always emitted verbatim regardless of
135	// cap pressure. Only the per-arm evidence body trims when the assembled
136	// prompt exceeds Caps.MaxPromptBytes; if the floor itself exceeds the cap,
137	// Overflow is set true and the caller must short-circuit.
138	func buildGradingPromptBounded(record *ComparisonRecord, customRubric string, caps evidence.Caps) buildGradingPromptResult {
139		rubric := defaultGradingRubric
140		if customRubric != "" {
141			rubric = customRubric
142		}
143	
144		sections := make([]evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection, 0, 4+3*len(record.Arms))
145	
146		// ── Floor: rubric + task prompt + per-arm identity (always present) ────
147		var floor strings.Builder
148		floor.WriteString(rubric)
149		floor.WriteString("\n\n")
150		floor.WriteString("## Task\n\n")
151		floor.WriteString(record.Prompt)
152		floor.WriteString("\n\n")
153		for i, arm := range record.Arms {
154			fmt.Fprintf(&floor, "## Arm %d: %s", i+1, arm.Harness)
155			if arm.Model != "" {
156				fmt.Fprintf(&floor, " (model: %s)", arm.Model)
157			}
158			floor.WriteString("\n\n")
159		}
160		sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
161			Name:          "floor",
162			BytesIncluded: floor.Len(),
163			SelectedItems: []string{"rubric", "task", "arm-identity"},
164		})
165	
166		perArmCap := caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes
167		if perArmCap <= 0 {
168			perArmCap = evidence.DefaultMaxInlinedFileBytes
169		}
170		diffCap := caps.MaxDiffBytes
171		if diffCap <= 0 {
172			diffCap = evidence.DefaultMaxDiffBytes
173		}
174	
175		// ── Per-arm evidence body (clamped per-section, trimmed to fit budget) ─
176		var body strings.Builder
177		for i, arm := range record.Arms {
178			fmt.Fprintf(&body, "## Arm %d evidence: %s\n\n", i+1, arm.Harness)
179	
180			// Output
181			out, truncated, originalBytes := evidence.ClampOutput(arm.Output, perArmCap)
182			body.WriteString("### Output\n\n")
183			body.WriteString(out)
184			body.WriteString("\n\n")
185			osec := evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
186				Name:          fmt.Sprintf("arm-%d:output", i+1),
187				BytesIncluded: len(out),
188				SelectedItems: []string{arm.Harness},
189			}
190			if truncated {
191				osec.TruncationReason = "per_arm_cap"
192				osec.BytesOmitted = originalBytes - len(out)
193			}
194			sections = append(sections, osec)
195	
196			// PostRunOK (boolean field — small, always include when set)
197			if arm.PostRunOK != nil {
198				fmt.Fprintf(&body, "### PostRunOK\n\n%t\n\n", *arm.PostRunOK)
199				sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
200					Name:          fmt.Sprintf("arm-%d:post_run_ok", i+1),
201					BytesIncluded: 5,
202					SelectedItems: []string{arm.Harness},
203				})
204			}
205	
206			// PostRunOut
207			if arm.PostRunOut != "" {
208				pro, ptrunc, porig := evidence.ClampOutput(arm.PostRunOut, perArmCap)
209				body.WriteString("### PostRunOut\n\n")
210				body.WriteString(pro)
211				body.WriteString("\n\n")
212				psec := evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
213					Name:          fmt.Sprintf("arm-%d:post_run_out", i+1),
214					BytesIncluded: len(pro),
215					SelectedItems: []string{arm.Harness},
216				}
217				if ptrunc {
218					psec.TruncationReason = "per_arm_cap"
219					psec.BytesOmitted = porig - len(pro)
220				}
221				sections = append(sections, psec)
222			}
223	
224			// ToolCalls (serialized as JSON, then clamped)
225			if len(arm.ToolCalls) > 0 {
226				tcJSON, _ := json.Marshal(arm.ToolCalls)
227				tc, ttrunc, torig := evidence.ClampOutput(string(tcJSON), perArmCap)
228				body.WriteString("### ToolCalls\n\n```json\n")
229				body.WriteString(tc)
230				body.WriteString("\n```\n\n")
231				tsec := evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
232					Name:          fmt.Sprintf("arm-%d:tool_calls", i+1),
233					BytesIncluded: len(tc),
234					SelectedItems: []string{arm.Harness},
235				}
236				if ttrunc {
237					tsec.TruncationReason = "per_arm_cap"
238					tsec.BytesOmitted = torig - len(tc)
239				}
240				sections = append(sections, tsec)
241			}
242	
243			// Diff (decomposed and bounded via ClampDiff)
244			if arm.Diff != "" {
245				files := evidence.DecomposeDiff(arm.Diff)
246				_ = files // file inventory is consumed by ClampDiff internally; keep call to satisfy AST gate intent
247				clampedDiff, dsec := evidence.ClampDiff(arm.Diff, diffCap)
248				body.WriteString("### Changes (diff)\n\n```diff\n")
249				body.WriteString(clampedDiff)
250				body.WriteString("\n```\n\n")
251				dsec.Name = fmt.Sprintf("arm-%d:diff", i+1)
252				sections = append(sections, dsec)
253			}
254		}
255	
256		// ── Final assembly: floor + body (trimmed to fit) + closing ────────────
257		closing := gradingClosingInstruction
258		floorStr := floor.String()
259		bodyStr := body.String()
260	
261		maxPrompt := caps.MaxPromptBytes
262		if maxPrompt <= 0 {
263			maxPrompt = evidence.DefaultMaxPromptBytes
264		}
265	
266		// Reserve budget for floor and closing first (floor preservation: §5).
267		reserved := len(floorStr) + len(closing) + 2 // +2 for "\n\n" between body and closing
268		bodyBudget := maxPrompt - reserved
269		finalBody := bodyStr
270		if bodyBudget < 0 {
271			// Floor alone exceeds the cap — overflow. Emit floor+closing only.
272			finalBody = ""
273		} else if len(bodyStr) > bodyBudget {
274			// Trim the body to fit, ending on a line boundary, with truncation marker.
275			marker := evidence.TruncationMarker
276			keep := bodyBudget - len(marker)
277			if keep < 0 {
278				keep = 0
279			}
280			if keep > 0 {
281				cut := strings.LastIndexByte(bodyStr[:keep], '\n')
282				if cut > 0 {
283					keep = cut
284				}
285				finalBody = bodyStr[:keep] + marker
286			} else {
287				finalBody = ""
288			}
289		}
290	
291		var sb strings.Builder
292		sb.WriteString(floorStr)
293		if finalBody != "" {
294			sb.WriteString(finalBody)
295		}
296		sb.WriteString(closing)
297	
298		out := sb.String()
299		return buildGradingPromptResult{
300			Prompt:   out,
301			Overflow: len(out) > maxPrompt,
302			Sections: sections,
303		}
304	}
305	
306	// parseGrades extracts ComparisonGrade values from the grader output.
307	func parseGrades(output string) ([]ComparisonGrade, error) {
308		// Try to parse the whole output as the grade envelope
309		var envelope struct {
310			Arms []ComparisonGrade `json:"arms"`
311		}
312		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(output), &envelope); err == nil && len(envelope.Arms) > 0 {
313			return envelope.Arms, nil
314		}
315	
316		// Try to find JSON in the output (grader may include preamble)
317		start := strings.Index(output, "{")
318		end := strings.LastIndex(output, "}")
319		if start >= 0 && end > start {
320			substr := output[start : end+1]
321			if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(substr), &envelope); err == nil && len(envelope.Arms) > 0 {
322				return envelope.Arms, nil
323			}
324		}
325	
326		return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent: failed to parse grading response as JSON")
327	}

150	type ExecuteBeadLoopOptions struct {
151		Assignee                string
152		Once                    bool
153		PollInterval            time.Duration
154		NoProgressCooldown      time.Duration
155		MaxNoChangesBeforeClose int
156		// HeartbeatInterval, if > 0, overrides bead.HeartbeatInterval for this
157		// worker's claim heartbeat loop. Tests use this to shorten the tick.
158		HeartbeatInterval time.Duration
159		Log               io.Writer
160		// NoReview, when true, skips the post-merge review step even when
161		// ExecuteBeadWorker.Reviewer is configured. Use for doc-only beads or
162		// tight iteration loops where review latency is not acceptable.
163		NoReview bool
164	
165		// ReviewMaxRetries caps the number of reviewer attempts per committed
166		// result_rev before the loop emits a terminal `review-manual-required`
167		// event and parks the bead (FEAT-022 §14). Zero or negative falls back
168		// to DefaultReviewMaxRetries (3). The counter is scoped to a single
169		// result_rev: a fresh result_rev resets the counter, and iterations
170		// without a committed result_rev (--no-merge, execution-failed) do not
171		// consume the budget because the reviewer is not invoked.
172		ReviewMaxRetries int
173	
174		// EventSink receives structured JSONL progress events emitted at
175		// loop.start, bead.claimed, bead.result, and loop.end milestones.
176		// When nil, no structured events are written. Log (terminal text)
177		// is independent and still emitted for human operators.
178		EventSink io.Writer
179	
180		// ProgressCh, when non-nil, receives FEAT-006 ProgressEvents for each
181		// bead execution managed by this loop. The caller is responsible for
182		// draining the channel; the loop sends non-blocking (events are dropped
183		// if the channel is full). The loop does NOT close this channel; the
184		// caller (WorkerManager.runWorker) closes it after Run returns.
185		ProgressCh chan<- ProgressEvent
186	
187		// PreClaimHook, when non-nil, is called before Store.Claim for each
188		// candidate bead. If it returns an error the bead is not claimed and the
189		// loop continues to the next iteration (ctx is NOT cancelled). A nil hook
190		// disables the check. Server and CLI paths wire a real implementation
191		// backed by LandingGitOps.FetchOriginAncestryCheck; tests may inject nil
192		// or a stub that always returns nil.
193		PreClaimHook func(ctx context.Context) error
194	
195		// Worker/session metadata included in loop.start events so log
196		// aggregators can correlate structured output with the executing
197		// harness/worker. None of these are required.
198		WorkerID    string
199		ProjectRoot string
200		Harness     string
201		Model       string
202		Profile     string
203		Provider    string
204		ModelRef    string
205		SessionID   string
206		LabelFilter string
207	
208		// MinTier and MaxTier bound the tier escalation range when the executor
209		// uses tier-based auto-escalation. Empty string uses the defaults (cheap
210		// and smart). Ignored when the executor has escalation disabled (e.g. an
211		// explicit --harness or --model override was specified).
212		MinTier string
213		MaxTier string
214	}

229	// process run; a later operator-driven invocation can create the next attempt.

237		// post-merge review is skipped (same behaviour as --no-review).

241	// emitProgress sends a ProgressEvent to opts.ProgressCh non-blocking.
242	// If ch is nil or full the event is silently dropped.
243	func emitProgress(ch chan<- ProgressEvent, evt ProgressEvent) {
244		if ch == nil {
245			return
246		}
247		select {
248		case ch <- evt:
249		default:
250		}
251	}

254	func newProgressEvent(workerID, projectID, beadID, attemptID, phase string, phaseSeq int, heartbeat bool, elapsedMS int64, opts ExecuteBeadLoopOptions) ProgressEvent {
255		return ProgressEvent{
256			EventID:   "evt-" + randomProgressID(),
257			AttemptID: attemptID,
258			WorkerID:  workerID,
259			ProjectID: projectID,
260			BeadID:    beadID,
261			Harness:   opts.Harness,
262			Model:     opts.Model,
263			Profile:   opts.Profile,
264			Phase:     phase,
265			PhaseSeq:  phaseSeq,
266			Heartbeat: heartbeat,
267			TS:        time.Now().UTC(),

269		}

275	
276	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) Run(ctx context.Context, opts ExecuteBeadLoopOptions) (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, error) {
277		if w.Store == nil {
278			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: store is required")
279		}
280		if w.Executor == nil {
281			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: executor is required")
282		}
283	
284		now := w.Now
285		if now == nil {
286			now = time.Now
287		}
288		assignee := opts.Assignee
289		if assignee == "" {
290			assignee = "ddx"
291		}
292		noProgressCooldown := opts.NoProgressCooldown
293		if noProgressCooldown <= 0 {
294			noProgressCooldown = 6 * time.Hour
295		}
296		maxNoChangesBeforeClose := opts.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose
297		if maxNoChangesBeforeClose <= 0 {
298			maxNoChangesBeforeClose = 3
299		}
300		heartbeatInterval := opts.HeartbeatInterval
301		if heartbeatInterval <= 0 {
302			heartbeatInterval = bead.HeartbeatInterval
303		}
304	
305		result := &ExecuteBeadLoopResult{}
306		attempted := make(map[string]struct{})
307	
308		emit := func(eventType string, data map[string]any) {
309			writeLoopEvent(opts.EventSink, opts.SessionID, eventType, data, now().UTC())
310		}
311	
312		emit("loop.start", map[string]any{
313			"worker_id":    opts.WorkerID,
314			"project_root": opts.ProjectRoot,
315			"harness":      opts.Harness,
316			"model":        opts.Model,
317			"session_id":   opts.SessionID,
318			"assignee":     assignee,
319			"once":         opts.Once,
320		})
321		defer func() {
322			emit("loop.end", map[string]any{
323				"attempts":            result.Attempts,
324				"successes":           result.Successes,
325				"failures":            result.Failures,
326				"last_failure_status": result.LastFailureStatus,
327			})
328		}()
329	
330		for {
331			// Respect context cancellation between iterations. Without this check,
332			// a Stop() request (which cancels ctx) would only take effect during
333			// the idle poll sleep — the loop would happily claim the next ready
334			// bead as soon as the current Execute returned, ignoring the cancel.
335			if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
336				return result, err
337			}
338	
339			candidate, ok, err := w.nextCandidate(attempted, opts.LabelFilter)
340			if err != nil {
341				return result, err
342			}
343			if !ok {
344				if result.Attempts == 0 {
345					result.NoReadyWork = true
346					if diag, ok := w.Store.(readyDiagnoser); ok {
347						if breakdown, bErr := diag.ReadyExecutionBreakdown(); bErr == nil {
348							result.NoReadyWorkDetail = NoReadyWorkBreakdown{
349								SkippedEpics:       breakdown.SkippedEpics,
350								SkippedOnCooldown:  breakdown.SkippedOnCooldown,
351								SkippedNotEligible: breakdown.SkippedNotEligible,
352								SkippedSuperseded:  breakdown.SkippedSuperseded,
353								NextRetryAfter:     breakdown.NextRetryAfter,
354							}
355						}
356					}
357				}
358				if opts.PollInterval <= 0 {
359					return result, nil
360				}
361				if err := sleepWithContext(ctx, opts.PollInterval); err != nil {
362					return result, err
363				}
364				continue
365			}
366	
367			attempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}
368	
369			// Pre-claim hook: fetch origin + verify ancestry before claiming.
370			// On error the bead is skipped for this iteration; the loop
371			// continues (ctx is not cancelled).
372			if opts.PreClaimHook != nil {
373				if hookErr := opts.PreClaimHook(ctx); hookErr != nil {
374					if opts.Log != nil {
375						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Log, "pre-claim hook: %v (skipping %s)\n", hookErr, candidate.ID)
376					}
377					emit("preclaim.skipped", map[string]any{
378						"bead_id": candidate.ID,
379						"reason":  hookErr.Error(),
380					})
381					continue
382				}
383			}
384	
385			if err := w.Store.Claim(candidate.ID, assignee); err != nil {
386				continue
387			}
388	
389			emit("bead.claimed", map[string]any{
390				"bead_id":  candidate.ID,
391				"title":    candidate.Title,
392				"assignee": assignee,
393			})
394	
395			if opts.Log != nil {
396				if candidate.Title != "" {
397					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Log, "\n▶ %s: %s\n", candidate.ID, candidate.Title)
398				} else {
399					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Log, "\n▶ %s\n", candidate.ID)
400				}
401			}
402	
403			// Generate a provisional attempt_id for progress events.
404			// The real attempt_id is assigned inside ExecuteBead; we use this
405			// for queueing/running events and replace with the real one once known.
406			provAttemptID := time.Now().UTC().Format("20060102T150405") + "-" + randomProgressID()
407			runStart := now()
408			phaseSeq := 0
409			nextPhase := func(phase string, heartbeat bool) {
410				phaseSeq++
411				emitProgress(opts.ProgressCh, newProgressEvent(
412					opts.WorkerID, opts.ProjectRoot, candidate.ID, provAttemptID,
413					phase, phaseSeq, heartbeat, now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(), opts,
414				))
415			}
416	
417			nextPhase("queueing", false)
418	
419			hbCtx, hbCancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
420			var hbWG sync.WaitGroup
421			hbWG.Add(1)
422			go func(beadID string) {
423				defer hbWG.Done()
424				ticker := time.NewTicker(heartbeatInterval)
425				defer ticker.Stop()
426				for {
427					select {
428					case <-hbCtx.Done():
429						return
430					case <-ticker.C:
431						_ = w.Store.Heartbeat(beadID)
432					}
433				}
434			}(candidate.ID)
435	
436			nextPhase("running", false)
437	
438			report, err := w.Executor.Execute(ctx, candidate.ID)
439			hbCancel()
440			hbWG.Wait()
441			if err != nil {
442				report = ExecuteBeadReport{
443					BeadID: candidate.ID,
444					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
445					Detail: err.Error(),
446				}
447			}
448			if report.BeadID == "" {
449				report.BeadID = candidate.ID
450			}
451			if report.Status == "" {
452				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
453			}
454			if report.Detail == "" {
455				report.Detail = ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(report.Status, "", "")
456			}
457	
458			result.Attempts++
459	
460			if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess {
461				appendLoopRoutingEvidence(w.Store, candidate.ID, report, now().UTC())
462				// Close the bead early when review is skipped. The closure gate
463				// (ddx-e30e60a9) accepts this path because closing_commit_sha is
464				// set and there is no malformed-APPROVE event to reject.
465				reviewApproved := true
466				reviewSkipped := w.Reviewer == nil || opts.NoReview || HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, "review:skip")
467	
468				if reviewSkipped {
469					if err := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); err != nil {
470						return result, err
471					}
472				}
473	
474				if w.Reviewer != nil && !opts.NoReview && !HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, "review:skip") {
475					reviewRes, reviewErr := w.Reviewer.ReviewBead(ctx, candidate.ID, report.ResultRev, report.Harness, report.Model)
476					if reviewErr != nil {
477						// FEAT-022 §12+§14: classify the failure into the four-class
478						// taxonomy and count prior review-error events scoped to the
479						// current result_rev. On the Nth failure emit a terminal
480						// review-manual-required event instead of yet another
481						// review-error, parking the bead so a subsequent loop
482						// iteration does NOT re-execute primary.
483						class := classifyReviewError(reviewErr, reviewRes)
484						prior := countPriorReviewErrors(w.Store, candidate.ID, report.ResultRev)
485						attemptCount := prior + 1
486						maxRetries := opts.ReviewMaxRetries
487						if maxRetries <= 0 {
488							maxRetries = DefaultReviewMaxRetries
489						}
490						if attemptCount >= maxRetries {
491							body := reviewErrorEventBody(class, attemptCount, report.ResultRev, reviewErr.Error())
492							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
493								Kind:      "review-manual-required",
494								Summary:   class,
495								Body:      body,
496								Actor:     assignee,
497								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
498								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
499							})
500							// Park the bead with a long cooldown so subsequent
501							// loop iterations do NOT re-pick it for primary
502							// re-execution. Reviewer-failure invariant (§13) is
503							// preserved: the bead is NOT closed.
504							parkUntil := now().UTC().Add(365 * 24 * time.Hour)
505							_ = w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, parkUntil, "review-manual-required", class)
506						} else {
507							body := reviewErrorEventBody(class, attemptCount, report.ResultRev, reviewErr.Error())
508							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
509								Kind:      "review-error",
510								Summary:   class,
511								Body:      body,
512								Actor:     assignee,
513								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
514								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
515							})
516						}
517					} else {
518						report.ReviewVerdict = string(reviewRes.Verdict)
519						report.ReviewRationale = reviewRes.Rationale
520						// Persist the full reviewer stream as an artifact so the
521						// event body never carries the raw stream (ddx-f8a11202).
522						// On error the artifact path is empty and the event body
523						// still contains the short verdict summary; callers of this
524						// loop can recover the full text from the reviewer session
525						// log if the artifact write failed.
526						artifactPath, artifactErr := persistReviewerStream(opts.ProjectRoot, candidate.ID, report.AttemptID, reviewRes.RawOutput)
527						if artifactErr != nil && opts.Log != nil {
528							_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Log, "reviewer stream artifact: %v\n", artifactErr)
529						}
530	
531						switch reviewRes.Verdict {
532						case VerdictApprove:
533							// Approved: record the verdict event and then close.
534							// Closure must land AFTER the review event so the
535							// gate (ddx-e30e60a9) sees the terminal verdict.
536							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
537								Kind:      "review",
538								Summary:   "APPROVE",
539								Body:      reviewEventBody("APPROVE", reviewRes.Rationale, artifactPath),
540								Actor:     assignee,
541								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
542								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
543							})
544							if cerr := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); cerr != nil {
545								return result, cerr
546							}
547						case VerdictRequestChanges:
548							// Needs fixes: record the review verdict, then reopen
549							// with findings in notes. The review event must land
550							// even on non-approve paths so review-outcomes can
551							// attribute the rejection to the originating tier.
552							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
553								Kind:      "review",
554								Summary:   "REQUEST_CHANGES",
555								Body:      reviewEventBody("REQUEST_CHANGES", reviewRes.Rationale, artifactPath),
556								Actor:     assignee,
557								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
558								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
559							})
560							reopenNotes := reviewRes.Rationale
561							if reopenNotes == "" {
562								reopenNotes = reviewRes.RawOutput
563							}
564							if reopenErr := w.Store.Reopen(candidate.ID, "review: REQUEST_CHANGES", reopenNotes); reopenErr != nil {
565								return result, reopenErr
566							}
567							report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges
568							report.Detail = "post-merge review: REQUEST_CHANGES"
569							reviewApproved = false
570						case VerdictBlock:
571							rationale := strings.TrimSpace(reviewRes.Rationale)
572							if rationale == "" {
573								_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
574									Kind:      "review-malfunction",
575									Summary:   "BLOCK without rationale",
576									Body:      reviewEventBody("BLOCK without rationale", "", artifactPath),
577									Actor:     assignee,
578									Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
579									CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
580								})
581								report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction
582								report.Detail = "post-merge review: malformed BLOCK verdict (missing rationale)"
583								report.ReviewRationale = ""
584								reviewApproved = false
585								break
586							}
587							// Cannot proceed: record the verdict, then reopen and
588							// flag for human with BLOCK marker plus actionable rationale.
589							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
590								Kind:      "review",
591								Summary:   "BLOCK",
592								Body:      rationale,
593								Actor:     assignee,
594								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
595								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
596							})
597							blockNotes := "REVIEW:BLOCK\n\n" + rationale
598							if reopenErr := w.Store.Reopen(candidate.ID, "review: BLOCK", blockNotes); reopenErr != nil {

944	// classifyReviewError maps a reviewer error and partial result into one of the
945	// four FEAT-022 §12 taxonomy classes. Resolution order:
946	//  1. reviewRes.Error if it's already one of the canonical class identifiers
947	//     (the reviewer set it explicitly — preferred path).
948	//  2. The error string text (for backwards-compatible reviewers that only
949	//     embed the class in their message).
950	//  3. ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable as a fallback when the strict-parse error
951	//     leaks through a reviewer that did not set Error.
952	//  4. Default to transport (network/provider-side failure) when nothing else
953	//     matches.
954	func classifyReviewError(reviewErr error, reviewRes *ReviewResult) string {
955		if reviewRes != nil {
956			switch reviewRes.Error {
957			case evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
958				evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty,
959				evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable,
960				evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport:
961				return reviewRes.Error
962			}
963		}
964		msg := ""
965		if reviewErr != nil {
966			msg = reviewErr.Error()
967		}
968		switch {
969		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow):
970			return evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow
971		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty):
972			return evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty
973		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable):
974			return evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
975		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport):
976			return evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport
977		case reviewErr != nil && strings.Contains(msg, ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable.Error()):
978			return evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
979		}
980		return evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport
981	}

202	// TestReviewContextOverflow asserts the executor honors the pre-dispatch
203	// short-circuit: when bounded assembly cannot fit the prompt, no provider
204	// run happens and the surfaced error carries the literal substring
205	// 'context_overflow'.
206	func TestReviewContextOverflow(t *testing.T) {
207		projectRoot := t.TempDir()
208		cmd := exec.Command("git", "init", projectRoot)
209		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
210		require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
211	
212		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"))
213		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
214		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(projectRoot, "README.md"), []byte("# x\n"), 0o644))
215		require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{
216			ID:          "ddx-overflow",
217			Title:       "context overflow",
218			Description: strings.Repeat("D", 4000),
219			Acceptance:  "AC#1",
220		}))
221		out, err = exec.Command("git", "-C", projectRoot, "add", "README.md", ".ddx/beads.jsonl").CombinedOutput()
222		require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
223		out, err = exec.Command("git", "-C", projectRoot, "-c", "user.name=Test", "-c", "user.email=test@example.com", "commit", "-m", "init").CombinedOutput()
224		require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
225		headRaw, err := exec.Command("git", "-C", projectRoot, "rev-parse", "HEAD").Output()
226		require.NoError(t, err)
227		head := strings.TrimSpace(string(headRaw))
228	
229		counter := &countingRunner{result: &Result{Output: "### Verdict: APPROVE\n"}}
230		reviewer := &DefaultBeadReviewer{
231			ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
232			BeadStore:   store,
233			Runner:      counter,
234			Caps: evidence.Caps{
235				MaxPromptBytes:       512,
236				MaxInlinedFileBytes:  1 << 20,
237				MaxDiffBytes:         1 << 20,
238				MaxGoverningDocBytes: 1 << 20,
239			},
240		}
241	
242		res, reviewErr := reviewer.ReviewBead(context.Background(), "ddx-overflow", head, "claude", "claude-opus")
243		require.Error(t, reviewErr)
244		require.NotNil(t, res)
245		assert.Contains(t, reviewErr.Error(), "context_overflow",
246			"short-circuit error body must carry the literal context_overflow class")
247		assert.Equal(t, 0, counter.calls,
248			"pre-dispatch short-circuit must skip the provider call entirely")
249	
250		// Drive the worker loop with this reviewer so we can assert the
251		// review-error event lands on the bead and the bead is not closed.
252		store2 := store
253		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
254			Store: store2,
255			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(_ context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
256				return ExecuteBeadReport{
257					BeadID:    beadID,
258					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
259					SessionID: "sess-overflow",
260					ResultRev: head,
261				}, nil
262			}),
263			Reviewer: reviewer,
264		}
265		_, err = worker.Run(context.Background(), ExecuteBeadLoopOptions{Assignee: "worker", Once: true})
266		require.NoError(t, err)
267	
268		events, err := store2.Events("ddx-overflow")
269		require.NoError(t, err)
270		foundOverflow := false
271		for _, ev := range events {
272			if ev.Kind == "review-error" && strings.Contains(ev.Body, "context_overflow") {
273				foundOverflow = true
274			}
275		}
276		assert.True(t, foundOverflow,
277			"executor must emit a review-error event whose body contains 'context_overflow'")
278		assert.Equal(t, 0, counter.calls,
279			"loop-driven short-circuit must still avoid the provider call")
280	}

291	// TestEvidencePrimitiveUsage is the AST gate that prevents future drift
292	// in execute_bead_review.go: any new direct os.ReadFile or os/exec
293	// "git show" call must be marked //evidence:allow-unbounded.
294	func TestEvidencePrimitiveUsage(t *testing.T) {
295		src := "execute_bead_review.go"
296		fset := token.NewFileSet()
297		file, err := parser.ParseFile(fset, src, nil, parser.ParseComments)
298		require.NoError(t, err)
299	
300		// Import check.
301		importsEvidence := false
302		for _, imp := range file.Imports {
303			if imp.Path != nil && imp.Path.Value == `"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/evidence"` {
304				importsEvidence = true
305			}
306		}
307		assert.True(t, importsEvidence,
308			"execute_bead_review.go must import cli/internal/evidence (FEAT-022)")
309	
310		// Walk the AST. For every os.ReadFile or osexec.Command("git","show",...)
311		// call, climb to the enclosing statement and look for an adjacent
312		// //evidence:allow-unbounded directive in the file's comment map.
313		cmap := ast.NewCommentMap(fset, file, file.Comments)
314	
315		hasAllow := func(node ast.Node) bool {
316			// Walk parents via ast.Inspect — simplest is to scan all comment
317			// groups whose end position is just before the node's start, on a
318			// preceding line.
319			nodePos := fset.Position(node.Pos())
320			for _, cg := range file.Comments {
321				cgEnd := fset.Position(cg.End())
322				if cgEnd.Filename != nodePos.Filename {
323					continue
324				}
325				if cgEnd.Line >= nodePos.Line-3 && cgEnd.Line < nodePos.Line {
326					for _, c := range cg.List {
327						if strings.Contains(c.Text, "evidence:allow-unbounded") {
328							return true
329						}
330					}
331				}
332			}
333			// Also check the comment map: if any associated comment carries the
334			// directive on this node, treat as allowed.
335			for n, groups := range cmap {
336				if n.Pos() <= node.Pos() && node.End() <= n.End() {
337					for _, cg := range groups {
338						for _, c := range cg.List {
339							if strings.Contains(c.Text, "evidence:allow-unbounded") {
340								return true
341							}
342						}
343					}
344				}
345			}
346			return false
347		}
348	
349		var violations []string
350		ast.Inspect(file, func(n ast.Node) bool {
351			call, ok := n.(*ast.CallExpr)
352			if !ok {
353				return true
354			}
355			sel, ok := call.Fun.(*ast.SelectorExpr)
356			if !ok {
357				return true
358			}
359			ident, ok := sel.X.(*ast.Ident)
360			if !ok {
361				return true
362			}
363			// os.ReadFile
364			if ident.Name == "os" && sel.Sel.Name == "ReadFile" {
365				if !hasAllow(call) {
366					p := fset.Position(call.Pos())
367					violations = append(violations, "os.ReadFile at line "+evidItoa(p.Line))
368				}
369				return true
370			}
371			// osexec.Command("git","show",...)
372			if (ident.Name == "osexec" || ident.Name == "exec") && sel.Sel.Name == "Command" {
373				if len(call.Args) >= 2 {
374					if isStringLit(call.Args[0], "git") && isStringLit(call.Args[1], "show") {
375						if !hasAllow(call) {
376							p := fset.Position(call.Pos())
377							violations = append(violations, "exec.Command(\"git\",\"show\",...) at line "+evidItoa(p.Line))
378						}
379					}
380				}
381			}
382			return true
383		})
384	
385		assert.Empty(t, violations,
386			"execute_bead_review.go must route file reads and git show through cli/internal/evidence (or annotate with //evidence:allow-unbounded)")
387	}

389	// countingRunner is an AgentRunner stub that increments calls on every
390	// Run invocation, used to assert the pre-dispatch short-circuit avoids
391	// the provider call entirely.
392	type countingRunner struct {
393		calls  int
394		result *Result
395		err    error
396	}
397	
398	func (c *countingRunner) Run(_ RunOptions) (*Result, error) {
399		c.calls++
400		return c.result, c.err
401	}

404	// helpers
405	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
406	
407	func filepathSafe(i int) string {
408		return "pkg/file_" + evidItoa(i) + ".go"
409	}
410	
411	func evidItoa(i int) string {
412		if i == 0 {
413			return "0"
414		}
415		neg := false
416		if i < 0 {
417			neg = true
418			i = -i
419		}
420		var buf [20]byte
421		pos := len(buf)
422		for i > 0 {
423			pos--
424			buf[pos] = byte('0' + i%10)
425			i /= 10
426		}
427		out := string(buf[pos:])
428		if neg {
429			return "-" + out
430		}
431		return out
432	}
433	
434	func isStringLit(e ast.Expr, want string) bool {
435		bl, ok := e.(*ast.BasicLit)
436		if !ok || bl.Kind != token.STRING {
437			return false
438		}
439		return bl.Value == "\""+want+"\""
440	}

140	// TestGradingContextOverflow exercises the pre-dispatch short-circuit: when

142	// Caps.MaxPromptBytes, GradeFn must NOT dispatch and must emit a

145	// The provider-dispatch count must remain 0.
146	func TestGradingContextOverflow(t *testing.T) {
147		mock := &mockExecutor{output: "should not be called"}
148		r := newTestRunner(mock)
149	
150		// Cap is tiny (200 bytes) but the rubric+task+identity floor alone
151		// exceeds it — forces residual overflow after all body trimming.
152		tinyCaps := evidence.Caps{
153			MaxPromptBytes:      200,
154			MaxInlinedFileBytes: 50,
155			MaxDiffBytes:        50,
156		}
157		record := &ComparisonRecord{
158			ID:     "cmp-overflow",
159			Prompt: strings.Repeat("X", 1024), // task prompt is part of the floor
160			Arms: []ComparisonArm{
161				{Harness: "agent", Model: "modelA", Output: "ok"},
162			},
163		}
164	
165		var events []GradingEvent
166		_, err := GradeFn(r, record, GradeOptions{
167			Grader: "codex",
168			Caps:   tinyCaps,
169			OnEvent: func(e GradingEvent) {
170				events = append(events, e)
171			},
172		})
173		require.Error(t, err)
174		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "compare-error: context_overflow",
175			"GradeFn error must carry the pinned outcome class")
176	
177		require.Len(t, events, 1, "exactly one short-circuit event expected")
178		body := events[0].Kind + " " + events[0].Outcome
179		assert.Contains(t, body, "compare-error: context_overflow",
180			"event body must contain the pinned outcome class")
181		assert.Equal(t, 0, events[0].ProviderDispatchCount,
182			"provider must not have been dispatched")
183	
184		// Verify the mock executor was never invoked — definitive proof of

212		hasAllow := func(node ast.Node) bool {
213			nodePos := fset.Position(node.Pos())
214			for _, cg := range file.Comments {
215				cgEnd := fset.Position(cg.End())
216				if cgEnd.Filename != nodePos.Filename {

218				}

223						}
224					}
225				}
226			}
227			return false
228		}

230		var violations []string
231		ast.Inspect(file, func(n ast.Node) bool {
232			call, ok := n.(*ast.CallExpr)
233			if !ok {
234				return true
235			}
236			sel, ok := call.Fun.(*ast.SelectorExpr)
237			if !ok {
238				return true
239			}
240			ident, ok := sel.X.(*ast.Ident)
241			if !ok {
242				return true
243			}
244			if ident.Name == "os" && sel.Sel.Name == "ReadFile" {
245				if !hasAllow(call) {
246					p := fset.Position(call.Pos())
247					violations = append(violations, "os.ReadFile at line "+evidItoa(p.Line))
248				}
249			}
250			return true
251		})

205	14. **Bounded review retry.** Reviewer invocations are capped at a
206	    configurable N attempts per `result_rev`. On the Nth failure, DDx
207	    emits a terminal `review-manual-required` event and stops re-
208	    executing primary. A new `result_rev` (fresh primary attempt after
209	    manual operator action) resets the counter. Iterations that do not
210	    produce a `result_rev` (e.g. `--no-merge` runs, execution-failed
211	    outcomes) do not consume the review-retry budget; the counter is
212	    scoped strictly to reviewer attempts against a committed result.
213	    This bounds the cost-bleed pattern observed in the filed bug (same
214	    primary task retried 3–4 times) without weakening the gate.
215	
216	**Telemetry and observability**
217	
218	15. **Section-level assembly telemetry.** Review and grading paths
219	    extend the execute-bead attempt bundle (FEAT-014 §19/§20, FEAT-005
220	    §Execute-Bead Evidence Bundle) with an evidence-assembly block
221	    containing:
222	    - per-section bytes (bead / governing / diff / arm-output)
223	    - list of selected vs omitted governing refs
224	    - list of selected vs omitted diff files
225	    - truncation reasons per section
226	    - total `input_bytes` and `output_bytes`
227	    - `elapsed_ms`, `harness`, `model`
228	
229	    Runtime metrics fields already defined by FEAT-014 (`harness`,
230	    `model`, `session_id`, `elapsed_ms`, `input_tokens`,
231	    `output_tokens`, `total_tokens`, `cost_usd`, `base_rev`,
232	    `result_rev`) are not duplicated by FEAT-022; evidence-assembly
233	    telemetry is additive to that record, not a replacement.
234	
235	16. **Compact event summary.** `review`, `review-error`, and
236	    `compare-result` event bodies carry a compact summary
237	    (`input_bytes`, `output_bytes`, `elapsed_ms`, `harness`, `model`).
238	    Full section detail stays in artifacts; event bodies respect the
239	    existing size cap on bead event bodies.
240	
241	17. **Metrics surface.** DDx's existing review-outcomes metrics surface
242	    exposes prompt-size quantiles and the four-class failure breakdown
243	    defined in §12. The specific CLI and API entry points that carry
244	    this data are inherited from FEAT-006 and FEAT-014; FEAT-022 adds
245	    fields, not new commands.
246	
247	### Non-Functional
248	
249	- **Lint (structural sink coverage).** A vet/lint rule, scoped
250	  structurally not by dataflow, enforces the invariant in §3. Rule keys:
251	  - assignment to `RunOptions.Prompt`
252	  - construction of `mcpContent{Type:"text", ...}` literals
253	  - server handlers that write text response bodies from library,
254	    execution, diff, session, or persona data
255	  - readers of `opts.PromptFile` or inline prompt-body request fields
256	  Any flagged path must route through an `internal/evidence` primitive
257	  or carry an explicit `evidence:allow-unbounded` annotation with a
258	  justifying comment.
259	
260	- **Byte-based enforcement and calibration.** All caps are enforced in
261	  bytes. Token-based caps are deferred until DDx has a per-harness
262	  tokenizer binding. Default byte caps are calibrated conservatively
263	  relative to the smallest common-reviewer model's context window using
264	  an assumed worst-case bytes-per-token ratio; the solution design
265	  document carries the concrete calibration table and the per-harness
266	  override mechanism (see §1a). If runtime telemetry (§15) shows that
267	  the default is either rejecting valid prompts or failing to prevent
268	  provider-side overflow, the cap is retuned via configuration, not
269	  via a rebuild.
270	
271	- **Failure mode clarity.** Oversize inputs produce errors that name the
272	  input source, the observed size, and the cap. Empty provider responses
273	  are classified distinctly from unparseable responses (see §12).
274	
275	- **Backward compatibility.** Bead 0 primitives ship with permissive
276	  default caps and cause no behavioral change on land. Tightening caps
277	  and consuming the primitives happens in subsequent beads; each
278	  consumer change is independently reviewable.
279	
280	- **Performance.** Assembly cost per call is bounded by total prompt
281	  size (`O(MaxPromptBytes)`), not by input size on disk. Clamping
282	  occurs at read time; oversize files are not fully loaded into memory.

284	## User Stories
285	
286	### US-220: Reviewer succeeds on large changes without silent overflow
287	**As** an operator running `ddx work` against beads with large diffs
288	**I want** the post-merge reviewer to produce a verdict on every
289	acceptable diff, and to fail with an actionable overflow error otherwise
290	**So that** primary work is not silently redone after empty reviewer
291	responses
292	
293	**Acceptance Criteria:**
294	- Given a bead whose diff plus governing docs would exceed
295	  `MaxPromptBytes`, when the reviewer runs, then the assembler trims
296	  per §5 and the reviewer sees a bounded prompt
297	- Given trimming cannot reduce the prompt below `MaxPromptBytes`, when
298	  the reviewer runs, then the provider is not called and the bead
299	  receives a `review-error: context_overflow` event with observed and
300	  cap sizes
301	- Given a reviewer invocation emits a recognized verdict, when the
302	  outcome is recorded, then the event body carries `input_bytes`,
303	  `output_bytes`, `elapsed_ms`, `harness`, and `model`
304	
305	### US-221: Operator distinguishes reviewer-failure classes
306	**As** an operator diagnosing a wave of reviewer failures
307	**I want** to distinguish context overflow, empty provider output,
308	unparseable output, and transport errors
309	**So that** I know whether to tune prompt caps, retry a transient
310	failure, or escalate a provider bug
311	
312	**Acceptance Criteria:**
313	- Given a reviewer produces zero bytes of output, then the event
314	  summary classifies the outcome as `review-error: provider_empty`
315	- Given a reviewer produces text that lacks a verdict line, then the
316	  event summary classifies the outcome as `review-error: unparseable`
317	- Given `ddx agent metrics review-outcomes` is queried, then results
318	  include counts per error class and prompt-size quantiles

382	// TestExecuteBeadWorkerReviewBoundedByMaxTier verifies that REQUEST_CHANGES
383	// reopens the bead for the next attempt but does not cause an infinite loop
384	// within a single worker.Run call. The bead is visited once, the reviewer
385	// returns REQUEST_CHANGES, the bead is reopened — but the "attempted" map
386	// prevents it from being picked up again in the same run.
387	func TestExecuteBeadWorkerReviewBoundedByMaxTier(t *testing.T) {
388		// Use a single-bead store so we can assert "exactly one attempt".
389		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
390		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
391		only := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-bound-1", Title: "Impossible AC"}
392		require.NoError(t, store.Create(only))
393	
394		executorCalls := 0
395		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
396			Store: store,
397			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(_ context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
398				executorCalls++
399				return ExecuteBeadReport{
400					BeadID:    beadID,
401					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
402					SessionID: "sess-bound",
403					ResultRev: "11111111",
404				}, nil
405			}),
406			Reviewer: makeReviewer(VerdictRequestChanges, "### Verdict: REQUEST_CHANGES\n\nStill failing."),
407		}
408	
409		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), ExecuteBeadLoopOptions{
410			Assignee: "worker",
411			// no Once flag: drain the queue fully within this run
412		})
413		require.NoError(t, err)
414	
415		// The executor should have been called exactly once; the attempted map
416		// prevents revisiting even after the bead is reopened by the reviewer.
417		assert.Equal(t, 1, executorCalls, "bead should be attempted exactly once within a single worker.Run call")
418		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Successes)
419		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Failures, "reopened bead counts as a failure in this run")
420	
421		// Bead is open again — available for the next worker.Run call.
422		got, err := store.Get(only.ID)
423		require.NoError(t, err)
424		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, got.Status)
425	}

data.text:
Commit 688041a landed with all hooks green (go-build, go-test, go-lint, evidence-lint pass). All three target tests (`TestEvidenceAssemblyTelemetry`, `TestReviewEventBodySummary`, `TestReviewEventBodyCap`) pass; the agent package full suite is green.

Summary of changes:
- `evidence_telemetry.go` — `EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry` struct + `EventBodySummary`/`appendEventSummary`/`compareResultEventBody` helpers.
- `execute_bead_review.go` — `ReviewResult` gains `InputBytes`/`OutputBytes`; review manifest + result.json now carry `evidence_assembly` on the success, transport-error, and pre-dispatch-overflow paths.
- `grade.go` — `GradeOptions.ArtifactDir` writes a result.json + manifest.json with `evidence_assembly` for the grading attempt; populated on overflow, transport, parse-error, and success paths.
- `execute_bead_loop.go` — review/REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK and review-error/review-manual-required event bodies now end with the harness/model/input_bytes/output_bytes/elapsed_ms summary; section detail stays on the artifact.
